headline: Observe sensations with curiosity
subtext: A way to teach your brain that a familiar symptom is not dangerous.
steps: Notice a mild symptom and turn gentle attention towards it.
Describe it plainly: where it is, its size, whether it shifts.
Remind yourself you are safe, and watch it with curiosity.
caption:
Observe sensations with curiosity. It sounds simple, but it is one of the most powerful retraining tools we have.

Somatic tracking works exactly this way. You turn calm, curious attention towards a sensation, describe it plainly, and remind yourself you are safe. Viewed as a safe, neutral signal rather than a threat, the feeling slowly loses the alarm the brain has attached to it.

In chronic pain, fibromyalgia, IBS, tinnitus and long COVID, much of the distress comes from the fear a symptom triggers. Watching it without panic helps break that loop.

It takes practice, and it is easier with guidance. Find a practitioner at https://neurowaze.health/start-my-recovery

#ChronicPain #Neuroplasticity #SomaticTracking #Fibromyalgia #IBS #Tinnitus #LongCovid #MindBody #PainRecovery #Neurowaze
first_comment: Ashar et al. (2022), Effect of Pain Reprocessing Therapy vs Placebo and Usual Care for Patients With Chronic Back Pain, JAMA Psychiatry, 79(1), 13 to 23. Somatic tracking is a core technique of this approach, which left two thirds of patients pain-free or nearly so. https://jamanetwork.com/journals/jamapsychiatry/fullarticle/2784694
